description: Illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing (fishery products). IPAFFS files these as CHED-P (fish); the new service splits them into a standalone IUU journey (catch certificate + IUU declaration). The IUU surface in this corpus is the single title-marked CHED-P Fish notification trace.

boundary.rule: Select CHED-P titles (title contains 'ched-p'/'chedp' AND NOT 'ched-pp'/'chedpp') that mention 'Fish'. This is the sharp edge: IUU has no title slug, and CHED-P fish is filed as CHED-P but is a distinct journey. Do NOT widen to all of CHED-P; do NOT return zero.
boundary.excluded_non_fish_ched_p: ~90 CHED-P traces excluded because they are products-of-animal-origin CHED-P (standard notifications, date validations, inspection non-transit, cloning, auto-clearance, replace/border notifications, POE/Holyhead, CUC, manipulation) plus shared document-upload/access specs tagged 'CHED-P' — none mention Fish, so none are IUU.
boundary.excluded_longer_slug: All 'ched-pp'/'chedpp' (CHED-PP plants) traces excluded as a longer, different type slug.
boundary.fold_in_note: During the later Extract/Comb wave, if a fish CN code (HS chapter 03) or a catch-certificate page appears inside another ched-p trace, fold that trace in as IUU and note it. At index time only the one Fish-titled trace qualifies.

traces.title: B2C Importer (Not Agent): Submits Valid CHEDP Fish Notification
traces.rationale: The only title-marked CHED-P Fish trace: a B2C importer submits a valid fishery-products notification end to end (193 actions, 12 pages, no errors) — the canonical IUU happy-path pre-notification.
